officialRules: Targets are chickens, pigs, turkeys, and rams.
Targets are set in groups of five.
Groups are engaged left to right.
Targets are engaged by distance order: chickens, pigs, turkeys, rams.
Target must be knocked down or pushed off stand to score.
courseOfFire: Engage silhouette banks in the required order.
Move to the next target after a miss.
Out-of-order hit is considered a miss.
instructionAuthority.intendedUse: Hit/miss silhouette competition or training with ordered animal targets at known distances.
passFailOrScoringRules: Score is number of hits per rounds fired.
Knockdown/off-stand is required to score on steel silhouette.
Tie-breakers may use reverse animal count or shoot-off depending on rule set.

geometryAuthority.gridSpacing: not applicable unless paper target uses grid
aimPointRules: Aim rules depend on animal target, distance, and firearm class.
scoringZones: Hit/knockdown zone is the silhouette body for steel targets.

minimumConfidenceRequirements: Backend must know silhouette variant before scoring.
Paper evidence requires geometry mapping for each animal target.
Steel knockdown scoring requires observed knockdown evidence or manual hit confirmation.

unknowns.neededSource: Target product or competition rule set specifying rifle/pistol/smallbore/airgun variant.
If supporting paper silhouette, official printed target dimensions and animal positions.

sourceAudit.notes: Used for general silhouette target family/course summary only; official rulebook needed before final authority scoring.
